titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Robert Rossen Papers PASC.0087
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by UCLA Library Special Collections staff; machine-readable finding aid created by D.MacGill.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated on 2026 March.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-18 11:19:00 -0700.

did.abstract: Collection consists of materials related to the career of American writer, producer, and director Robert Rossen.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.
did.langmaterial: Materials are primarily in English, with select materials in French and Japanese.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Reproduction and Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Robert Rossen papers (Collection PASC 87). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by UCLA Library Special Collections staff.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biographical History
bioghist.p: Rossen was born in New York, NY, Mar. 16, 1908; he began his career for the NY stage; moved to Hollywood (1939) as a contract writer for Warner Brothers and by the late 1940s was firmly established as the creator of some of America's most acclaimed films; his involvement with the Communist Party led to a subpoena from the House Un-American Activities Committee (1947); after "naming names" (1953), Rossen was allowed to continue working, but never returned to Hollywood, choosing instead to work in other locations; he won an Academy Award for Best Picture (1949) with All the King's Men; his numerous credits include such films as The Roaring Twenties (1939), The Hustler, and Lilith (both 1964); died Feb. 18, 1966.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of materials related to the career of American writer, producer, and director Robert Rossen. Includes various versions of scripts, notes and research material, clippings, stills, and posters. Includes material from numerous projects including the motion pictures Alexander The Great (1956), All The King's Men (1949), Body And Soul (1947), The Brave Bulls (1950), They Came To Cordura (1959), Edge Of Darkness (1942), The Hustler (1961), Lilith (1964), and The Sea Wolf (1941), and the stage plays The Body Beautiful, The Cool World, Corner Pocket, and a project titled Cocoa Beach. Includes some personal photographs.

controlaccess.occupation: Motion picture producers and directors -- United States -- Archives.

did.unittitle: Alexander the Great (1956): Location stills and snapshots

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Location stills and snapshots of R. Rossen, Richard Burton, Claire Bloom, Danielle Derrieux and others. 40 items of miscellaneous sizes.

did.unittitle: Synopsis of novel "A Lion is in the Streets" by Adria Locke Langley,attached Readers Report from Columbia Pictures, 7/26/44.
Synopsis of play "All The King's Men" by Robert Penn Warren,includes Columbia Pictures Inter-Office memos. 1946-1947. 5 items.
Synopsis of "All The King's Men" (no author, no date) by Shirley Miller. 32 pages.
Handwritten notes (unsorted, unidentified by scene), 250 pieces. n.d.
Handwritten and typed notes for treatment and screenplay by Robert Rossen, n.d. approx. 175 pgs.
Breakdown of script, 2 copies, 4 pgs. each. Script concordance, 5 pgs. Index and recommendations, 3 pgs. n.d.
3rd revised final draft of script, 3/1/49. Copy 1, bound, 140 pgs.
3rd revised final draft of script, 3/1/49. Copy 2, bound. 140 pgs.
Results of preview cards, Pasadena, CA., 6/16/49.
Bound copy of script: "Screenwriter's Guild Award to R.Rossen for..the best written drama of 1949". 3rd copy of 3rd revised final shooting script, 3/1/49.
Clipping from "Box Office", n.d. re: Rossen awarded tribute from Screen Directors Guild.
Scene stills (6 items 8"x10", 9 items 4"x5" ): R.R. rehearsing cast (6 items), Unidentified award (1 items), Oscar Awards (1 item 4"x5") R.R., Broderick Crawford, Mercedes McCambridge.
Poster/ads with variations in text and picture illustration, in color, 30 items. Press book. Plastic print mockup for billing.
Blues in the Night (1941): Script titled "Hot Nocturne"
Final shooting script revised, dated 1/13/47. Copy 1.
Final shooting script revised, datd 1/13/47. Copy 2.
Reviews: Washington D.C. Evening Star 11/13/47, Variety 8/13/47, N.Y. Times 11/10/47, Sunday News 11/9/47, L.A. Herald Examiner 11/22/47, Washington Post 11/13/47, Box Office 8/23/47.
Reviews (clippings), incl. interviews with cast members, Jan.- May, 1947.
Revised final draft of shooting script with tabs indexing scenes, dated 2/16/50.
Shooting script #6 with tabs indexing scenes, dated 10/26/49.
R. Rossen and cast on location, incl. Mel Ferrer, Anthony Quinn (121 items, 8"x10").
Dust Be My Destiny (1939): Final script
Handwritten script notes,including telephone dictation (4/25/61) and correspondence from Sidney Carroll (n.d.)
Bound copy of second draft, dated 7/6/60.
Book of set design sketches autographed by Harry Horner, dated 5/11/61.
Stills of R.Rossen, Paul Newman, Jackie Gleason, P. Laurie, G. C.Scott and others (41 items 8"x10").
Breakdown of book (n.d.), breakdown of picnic scene (n.d.)
Notes on "Lilith" by Dr. Charles Fischer, dated 1/17/63. 3 copies.
Notes dated 4/27/62-11/5/62, includes pages from the novel.
Bound final script, Rosson's copy, dated 1/10/63. copy 2.
"The Legend of Lilith" (n.d.),a biography of Robert Rossen (n.d.) and publicity campaign notes.
Location stills R. Rossen, Warren Beatty, Jean Seberg, Peter Fonda and others(80 items 8"x10").
Novel "They Came To Cordura" by Glendon Swarthout Random House, 1958 with indexing tabs.
They Won't Forget (1937): Script titled "In the Deep South"
Magazine articles:"Esquire" n.d., "The New Yorker" 2/15/58 and "The Insider", approx. 1962.
Synopsis for "Cocoa Beach", 4 copies dated 12/11/61, 2 undated.
Outline "#10 - A Film by R. Rossen", dated 12/1/64.
Handwritten notes, 453 pieces. Folder 1 of 2. n.d.
Handwritten notes, 453 pieces. Folder 2 of 2. n.d.
Handwritten pages, 359 pieces. Folder 1 of 2. n.d.
Handwritten pages, 359 pieces. Folder 2 of 2. n.d.
Handwritten sequence 19 pages and notebook in shorthand, n.d.
Notes on Cape Canaveral scene. 3 copies. n.d.
Sequence #3-Slattery, dated 3/12/65. Sequences After Slattery. 1 piece. n.d.
Sequence #2, dated 3/15/65. Notes on Opening Sequence. 1 piece. n.d.
Misc. notes and scenes. 96 pieces. n.d.
Misc. partial copies of notes and scripts. 17 copies. n.d.
Working script, misc. pages. 33 pieces. n.d.
Working script partial copies and misc. scenes. 177 pieces. (n.d.)
Final draft of treatment and screenplay, indexed. 2 copies. (n.d.)
Newspaper clippings from New York Post: 1/11/62, 11/6/62, 11/8/62, 11/15/62, 11/14/62.
The Cool World: Bound copy of play by Warren Miller and Robert Rossen
The Cool World: Bound copy of play by Warren Miller and Robert Rossen
Corner Pocket: 6 copies of partial stage script
"The Director", [essay by R.R.], 4 copies. (n.d.). [found w/COCOA BEACH material.
Letter from CENTAUR ENTERPRISES to R.R., dated 7/21/65 re office key and an appointment.
Articles about R.R. and his films: Film Quarterly, vol. 20 #2 (Winter 1966-67). Cahiers du Cinema, Jan. 1967, #7. Cinema, nos.6-7, Aug. 1970.
Newspaper notices on R.R.'s production plans, 1947-1964.
